name: Muhammad Mostafa Monowar
title: Professor, Department of Information Technology, King Abdulaziz University, Saudi Arabia
topic: Artificial Intelligence at an Inflection Point: Recent Advances and the Research Frontiers Ahead
bio: Muhammad Mostafa Monowar is a Professor in the Department of Information Technology at King AbdulAziz University, Saudi Arabia. He received his Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyung Hee University, South Korea, in 2011. His research interests include artificial intelligence, wireless sensor networks, Internet of Things (IoT), cybersecurity, privacy, and cloud computing. He has published over 70 peer-reviewed research articles with more than 2,200 citations, an h-index of 26, and an i10-index of 46. He has led numerous externally funded research projects and received several prestigious awards, including Best Paper Awards.

name: Prof. Dr. Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
title: Professor, Department of CSE, CUET; President, Bangladesh Computer Society
topic: Green AI for Sustainable Education: Building Intelligent, Inclusive, and Energy-Efficient Learning Ecosystems
bio: Prof. Dr. Mohammad Shamsul Arefin is a Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at CUET, Director of the Center for Intelligent Computing, and President of Bangladesh Computer Society. He previously served as Dean of the Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Chair of the IEEE Computer Society Bangladesh Chapter. He received his Doctor of Engineering from Hiroshima University, Japan. He has authored over 270 scholarly publications and was CUET’s highest publication contributor for three consecutive years. In 2025, he was listed among Bangladesh’s top 15 publication contributors. He has delivered more than 65 keynote and invited talks and led numerous national-level academic, research, and digital transformation initiatives.

name: Md Atiqur Rahman Ahad
title: Professor of AI, University of East London, UK; Visiting Professor, KyuTech, Japan
bio: Md Atiqur Rahman Ahad, Ph.D. (SMIEEE, SMOPTICA) is Professor of AI at the University of East London and Visiting Professor at Kyushu Institute of Technology. He works on AI in healthcare (Parkinson’s Disease, antimicrobial resistance, gait, rehabilitation) using vision and sensors, with a focus on AI safety and ethics. He has 60+ awards/grants, 21 books, and 220+ peer-reviewed publications. He is an Associate Editor of Pattern Recognition, Editorial Board Member of Scientific Reports (Nature), and General Chair of the 9th Int. Conf. on Activity and Behavior Computing (ABC) 2027. He serves on IEEE/ACM journals and conferences.

name: Dr. Shashikant Patil
title: Director and Professor, Atlas SkillTech University, Mumbai, India
bio: Dr. Shashikant Patil is a Full Professor and Senior Director at Atlas SkillTech University, Mumbai, with over 28 years of experience. He is a Senior Member of IEEE and ACM, Fellow of IETE, IEI, IBE, and ISDS, and a Chartered Engineer. His expertise spans Signal & Image Processing, Automation & Robotics, Data Science, Analytics, AI & Machine Learning. He has 103+ publications, seven books, one patent, and has reviewed over 5,000 research articles. He is an IEEE Brand Ambassador Expert and IEEE Computer Society Distinguished Visitor for 2025.

name: Dr. Md. Jakir Hossen
title: Associate Professor, Department of Robotics and Automation, Multimedia University (MMU), Malaysia
topic: A Modified Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference Engine and Its Applications
bio: Dr. Md. Jakir Hossen (SMIEEE, MIET, P.Tech) is an Associate Professor at Multimedia University (MMU), Malaysia. With over 20 years of experience, he specialises in Artificial Intelligence, Data Analytics, and their applications in Robotics, Healthcare, Renewable Energy, and IoT. He obtained his PhD from Universiti Putra Malaysia with a perfect CGPA of 4.00/4.00. He has authored over 150 journal publications, numerous conference papers, books, and patents. A Senior Member of IEEE, he has supervised many postgraduate students and received multiple international awards, including Best Researcher Award and Best Paper Awards.

name: Dr. Tangina Sultana
title: Associate Professor, Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Hajee Mohammad Danesh Science and Technology University, Dinajpur, Bangladesh
topic: Shaping the Future of Education: AI, Innovation, and Emerging Technologies
bio: Dr. Tangina Sultana, SMIEEE, is an Associate Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Hajee Mohammad Danesh Science and Technology University (HSTU), Dinajpur, Bangladesh. She holds a Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyung Hee University, South Korea, where she also served as a postdoctoral researcher. Her research spans graph neural networks, federated learning, explainable AI, edge computing, big data, and IoT, with over 60 publications. She is a granted Korean patent holder, editor of IntechOpen volume and Biztech Bytes magazine. A recipient of the IEEE Computer Society Bangladesh Chapter's Women in Computing Award 2025, she serves as Rangpur Divisional Coordinator and across multiple IEEE conference committees. She is also the organizing chair of ICBISET 2026.

name: Rashed Mazumder, Ph.D
title: Associate Professor, Institute of Information Technology, Jahangirnagar University, Bangladesh
topic: The Future of Cybersecurity: Explainable AI and Federated Learning for Intelligent Threat Detection
bio: Rashed Mazumder, Ph.D is an Associate Professor at the Institute of Information Technology (IIT), Jahangirnagar University, Bangladesh. He earned his Bachelor’s in CSE from the University of Dhaka and completed both his Master’s and Ph.D. at the Japan Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (JAIST). He holds Microsoft Certified IT Professional (MCITP) and Cisco CCNA certifications. With extensive industry experience, he provided a variety of IT professional services before joining academia. His interests include computer networks, cybersecurity, and emerging computing technologies.

name: Dr. Fida Hasan
title: Senior Lecturer in Cybersecurity, University of New South Wales (UNSW), Australia
topic: Trust, the Weakest Link in AI: What Does It Mean for AI to Be 'Trustworthy'?
bio: Dr. Fida Hasan is a Bangladeshi-Australian computer scientist and Senior Lecturer in Cybersecurity at UNSW. With over 18 years of academic experience, his research focuses on Trustworthy AI for Cybersecurity and security challenges in the era of quantum computing. He is a Harvard-certified higher education teacher, a Fellow of the Higher Education Academy (FHEA), a Senior Member of IEEE, and a professional member of the Australian Information Security Association (AISA).

name: Ajmal Hinas
title: Head, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, South Eastern University of Sri Lanka
topic: AI-First Software Engineering: Rethinking How We Build
bio: Dr. Ajmal Hinas is a computer engineer and researcher specializing in AI-driven software engineering, software quality, and automation. He is the Head of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the South Eastern University of Sri Lanka. His recent work focuses on making software development more reliable through approaches such as Test-Driven Requirements Engineering (TDRE). He holds multiple international patent applications and is passionate about democratizing automation for real-world impact.

name: Salheen Bakhet
title: CEO, SiliconicPro International, Pakistan
bio: Salheen Bakhet is a Gold Medalist, Harvard Scholar, and Young Global Leader in Science & Technology. With decades of experience in Data Sciences and AI, he has served Fortune 100 companies including Hewlett-Packard, Glaxo SmithKline, and CISCO. He is an accredited coder of IEEEXtreme and currently serves on the ExCom of IEEE Computer Society Lahore Section. He has been ranked as the top freelancer from Pakistan and has completed several hundred projects for clients in over 30 countries. He has also worked as a technical consultant for World Bank IT & reforms projects.

name: Dr. Mohammad Polash
title: Lecturer and Director of Teaching & Learning, School of Computer Science, The University of Sydney, Australia
bio: Dr. Mohammad Polash is a Lecturer at the University of Sydney, where he also serves as Director of Teaching & Learning. He has received multiple Best Paper Awards and the Vice-Chancellor's Award for Teaching and Learning Excellence. His research interests include Computing Education Research, Combinatorial Optimisation, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). He publishes extensively in high-impact journals and serves as a reviewer for top-tier conferences and journals.

name: Dr. Tahera Hossain
title: Research Assistant Professor, Nagoya University, Japan
topic: Human Behavior Modeling for Human-Centered AI: From Wearable Sensing to Real-World Health and Wellbeing
bio: Dr. Tahera Hossain is a Research Assistant Professor at Nagoya University, Japan, specializing in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and AI-driven healthcare technologies. She earned her Ph.D. in Engineering from the Kyushu Institute of Technology, Japan, in 2021. She has held research positions at the University of Michigan, Aoyama Gakuin University, and now Nagoya University. Her work leverages multimodal data (behavioral, physiological, clinical) and machine learning to understand human behaviour and predict health outcomes. She is an active member of ACM and ACM SIGCHI.

name: Minhaz-Us-Salakeen Fahme
title: Chairman & CEO, Battery Low Interactive Ltd., Bangladesh
topic: The Workforce of 2030: AI Analytics, Productivity Sweet Spots, and the End of the Eight-Hour Day
bio: Minhaz-Us-Salakeen Fahme is CEO and co-founder of Battery Low Interactive Ltd, Bangladesh’s pioneering interactive and XR studio. Since 2015, he has grown the company to 300+ paid solutions, 250+ clients, and a 50+ person team. He is also Managing Director of Sleepy Penguin Studios Ltd. He builds AI products at the intersection of technology and accessibility, including FeedBoss AI and Brand Review AI. Fahme is founding president of IGDA Bangladesh, General Secretary of the IUT Alumni Association, and the first Bangladeshi author of a globally published game development book (How to Make a Game, Apress/Springer Nature, 2021). A US State Department Professional Fellow, he has spoken at 40+ stages worldwide.
